excerpt: Safety Violent crime, such as assaults, armed robberies and thefts occur throughout the country. Petty crime, such as pickpocketing and bag snatching, occurs in Uruguay, especially in Montevideo. Crime throughout the country increases in summer (December to March).

advisory_level: Level 2 — Exercise Increased Caution Reissued after periodic review with changes
excerpt: Uruguay Travel Advisory --> Worldwide Caution: The Department of State advises Americans worldwide to exercise increased caution. Visas Intercountry Adoption International Parental Child Abduction Replace or Certify Documents Travel Advisories English Travel Advisories Travel.State.Gov > Travel Advisories > Uruguay Travel Advisory --> Share this page: All Filter Countries Uruguay Travel Advisory Travel Advisory May 7, 2025 Uruguay - Level 2: Exercise Increased Caution Reissued after periodic review with changes. Country Summary: Crime is most common in these departments: Montevideo Canelones Maldonado Rivera Violent crimes, such as homicides, armed robberies, car jackings, and thefts occur throughout the country. Do not physically resist any robbery attempt or try to stop a robbery in progress.

source_name: UK FCDO Travel Advice — Safety and Security
excerpt: Safety and security - Uruguay travel advice - GOV.UK Cookies on GOV.UK We use some essential cookies to make this website work. Accept additional cookies Reject additional cookies View cookies Hide cookie message Skip to main content Foreign travel advice Uruguay Subscriptions Get email alerts Safety and security Terrorism There is a high threat of terrorist attack globally affecting UK interests and British nationals, including from groups and individuals who view the UK and British nationals as targets. Find out how to reduce your risk from terrorism while abroad . Theft from cars in Montevideo Criminals regularly break into cars left on the street. Crime outside Montevideo Crime rates are generally lower in other parts of Uruguay, but still take usual precautions. Outdoor activities and adventure tourism Swimming safety Many beaches in Uruguay have lifeguards from December to March, although times vary across the country. Transport risks Road travel If you are planning to drive in Uruguay, see information on driving abroad . Extreme weather and natural disasters The Uruguay government usually issues warnings and updates on extreme weather through news channels and the press.
